origin.name: Gambia
origin.key_document: Death certificate from National Registration Authority
origin.source: FCDO Gambia travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Banjul guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Gambia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from National Registration Authority
special_rules: Gambia is popular with older British tourists and has a significant British retired community. The High Commission handles a notable volume of repatriation cases.
Police clearance is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Gambian authority is required for all repatriations.
Documentation is in English, which simplifies UK acceptance.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Gambian documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Banjul can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Gambia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Gambia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. National Registration Authority, police clearance, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
